displayTitle: Immersion Testing: Water Does the Work

beats.narration: In immersion testing, the probe never touches the part. Sound travels down a water path; the water is the couplant.

payload.title: IMMERSION TESTING
payload.sub: Sound coupled through a water column — no contact.

beats.narration: With no probe pressure, coupling never varies. Perfect for automated scanning. Tilt to steer, focus to a fine spot, map flaws with C scans.
The pulse bounces in the water, creating a second front surface echo. Too short a path, and it hides the back wall.

payload.heading: Multiples can hide the back wall
payload.note: TOO SHORT → MULTIPLE HIDES THE BACK WALL

beats.narration: Steel is four times faster than water, so the water path only needs a quarter of the thickness, plus a six millimeter margin.
Lock in the steel rule: water path, a quarter of the thickness, plus a margin. Contact for the field, immersion for the shop floor.

payload.sub: Contact for the field — immersion for the shop floor.
